description_long: This is a large, irregularly shaped stone with a rugged, angular form and a predominantly dark gray coloration. A prominent white mineral vein runs across one face, creating a striking visual contrast and suggesting a natural geological formation. The surface is rough and uneven, with jagged edges and a matte finish, indicating exposure to the elements. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or openings. Its size and mass would make it a significant obstacle or barrier in an urban environment, such as a park or roadside. The weathering and mineral banding give it a distinctive, natural appearance that stands out against more uniform urban materials.
description: A large, dark gray stone with a rough surface and a prominent white mineral vein running across one side. Its jagged, angular shape and natural weathering make it visually striking.
description_view_0: This view shows the stone's broad, uneven face with a mix of dark gray tones and subtle surface texture.
description_view_1: The white mineral vein is most visible here, curving across the stone's side and contrasting with the darker rock.
description_view_2: A flat face dominated by the horizontal white vein, highlighting the stone's layered appearance.
description_view_3: A side angle revealing the stone's thickness and the continuation of the mineral vein along the edge.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or embedded in the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular white mineral vein running across one or more faces, with natural mottling and layering in the gray stone.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered, with visible surface texture and minor edge rounding from prolonged exposure.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved surfaces but can blend with natural landscaping elements; may be partially surrounded by grass or soil.
